sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Follow-up for adult hydrocephalus and related symptoms.
History of Present Illness
Mr. Watson presents for follow-up regarding adult hydrocephalus, diagnosed in June 2020 following shunt surgery on August 1st, 2020. He reports improved walking ability since surgery, now able to walk to the dining room, though he still requires assistance at times. His bladder control has improved but remains managed with diapers. He reports improved cognition, stating he can now have normal conversations again. He denies headaches or pain at the shunt site. He has not experienced any recent hospitalizations related to the shunt.
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Physical exam reveals blood pressure of 124/80, heart rate of 64, respirations of 18, and a weight of 174 pounds. The shunt site is clean, dry, and intact with a setting of 2.0. Cognitive testing via MMSE with spelling yielded a score of 26/30 and with calculations 25/30, with two of three memory words recalled with cuing and one with multiple choice. Gait testing using the Tinetti assessment tool resulted in a gait score of 6-8/12 and a balance score of 12/16, for a total score of 18-20/28. CT scan from 10/15/2020 reveals a frontal horn span of 4.6 cm at the foramen of monro and a flat third ventricular contour of 10 mm. A small amount of blood is present in the right parietal region with a subdermal collection. The radiologist did not note any extra-axial fluid collections or significant vessel changes.
Assessment
The patient has adult hydrocephalus with ongoing gait impairment, urinary incontinence, and cognitive impairment. A small blood fluid collection was noted on the CT scan, but does not appear to be causing symptoms.
Plan
The shunt setting will remain unchanged for the next two to three months. Follow-up is scheduled in three months to reassess symptoms and imaging. The patient will return to the outpatient clinic for follow-up. The patient's daughter has been instructed to coordinate the appointment.
